Bash(python -c "
import sys, json
data = json.load(sys.stdin)

# Find Contact object node
contact_nodes = [n for n in data[''''nodes''''] if n.get(''''type'''') == ''''object'''' and ''''Contact'''' in n.get(''''label'''', '''''''')]

if contact_nodes:
    contact = contact_nodes[0]
    print(f''''=== CONTACT OBJECT ==='''')
    print(f''''  Label: {contact[""label""]}'''')
    
    props = contact.get(''''properties'''', {})
    fields = props.get(''''fields'''', [])
    print(f''''  Total fields: {len(fields)}'''')
    
    if fields:
        system_fields = [f for f in fields if f.get(''''isSystem'''')]
        explicit_fields = [f for f in fields if not f.get(''''isSystem'''')]
        
        if system_fields:
            print(f''''\n  System-required fields ({len(system_fields)}):'''')
            for f in system_fields:
                print(f''''    - {f[""name""]:30}'''')
        
        if explicit_fields:
            print(f''''\n  Explicit field permissions ({len(explicit_fields)}):'''')
            for f in explicit_fields:
                print(f''''    - {f[""name""]:30} (R:{f[""canRead""]}, E:{f[""canEdit""]})'''')
")

Bash(python -c "
import sys, json
data = json.load(sys.stdin)

# Find Contact object node (exactly ''''Contact'''', not ContactPointPhone)
contact_nodes = [n for n in data[''''nodes''''] if n.get(''''type'''') == ''''object'''' and n.get(''''id'''') == ''''object_Contact'''']

if contact_nodes:
    contact = contact_nodes[0]
    print(f''''=== CONTACT OBJECT ==='''')
    
    props = contact.get(''''properties'''', {})
    fields = props.get(''''fields'''', [])
    print(f''''Total fields: {len(fields)}'''')
    
    if fields:
        system_fields = [f for f in fields if f.get(''''isSystem'''')]
        explicit_fields = [f for f in fields if not f.get(''''isSystem'''')]
        
        if explicit_fields:
            print(f''''\nExplicit field permissions ({len(explicit_fields)}):'''')
            for f in explicit_fields[:15]:
                print(f''''  - {f[""name""]:30} (R:{f[""canRead""]}, E:{f[""canEdit""]})'''')
")

Bash(git commit -m "fix: Remove scrollbar from collapsed sidebar

Hide the scrollbar in the collapsed sidebar navigation while keeping
scroll functionality intact. This provides a cleaner look when the
sidebar is collapsed to 64px width.

Changes:
- Add scrollbar-hide utility class to sidebar navigation
- Add CSS utilities in globals.css to hide scrollbars cross-browser:
  - -ms-overflow-style: none (IE/Edge)
  - scrollbar-width: none (Firefox)
  - ::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none } (Chrome/Safari)

Maintains all sidebar functionality (hover expand, navigation, sync buttons)
while improving visual cleanliness in collapsed state.

Bash(git commit -m "fix: Show profile names for profile-owned permission sets in Fields tab

Profile-owned permission sets have auto-generated names like 
''X00e1a000000N1wLAAS'' which look like IDs. These are confusing
for users viewing field permissions.

Changes:
- Check if permission set is_owned_by_profile
- If yes, query for the associated profile
- Display as ''{ProfileName} (Profile)'' instead of auto-generated name
- Improves readability in Fields tab permission set list

Fixes issue where Fields detail page showed permission set IDs
instead of human-readable names.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>")

Bash(git commit -m "fix: Use correct snake_case field names for sync job timestamps

Dashboard was showing ''Invalid Date'' because it was trying to access
latestSync.completedAt and latestSync.startedAt, but the API returns
snake_case field names (completed_at, started_at).

Changes:
- Update dashboard page to use completed_at and started_at
- Update SyncJob TypeScript interface to match API response format
- Add metadata field to SyncJob interface
- Change all fields to snake_case for consistency with API

Fixes ''Invalid Date'' display in dashboard sync status banner.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>")
